usage_notes: Used predicatively (ich bin krank) and attributively (ein kranker Mann).
common_collocations: krank sein, krank werden, sich krank fühlen
image_search_query: person sick in bed with fever
image_context: A person lying in bed with a thermometer looking ill
example_2: Sie ist krank und bleibt zu Hause.
example_2_translation: Ela está doente e fica em casa.
example_3: Mein Bruder war letzte Woche sehr krank.
example_3_translation: Meu irmão esteve muito doente na semana passada.
pronunciation_tips: The 'r' is a uvular /ʁ/; 'a' is short as in 'cat'.
memory_hook: Cranky people often feel KRANK (sick).
related_words: der Arzt, das Krankenhaus, die Medizin, gesund
common_mistakes: Forgetting adjective endings when used attributively.

usage_notes: Refers specifically to a male doctor; female form is Ärztin.
common_collocations: zum Arzt gehen, den Arzt aufsuchen, beim Arzt sein
image_context: A doctor in a white coat holding a stethoscope in a clinic
example_2: Der Arzt sagt, ich brauche Ruhe.
example_2_translation: O médico diz que preciso de descanso.
example_3: Mein Arzt ist sehr nett.
example_3_translation: Meu médico é muito simpático.
pronunciation_tips: The final 'zt' cluster sounds like /tst/; stress on the first syllable.
memory_hook: An ARZT is the ARTist of healing.
related_words: die Ärztin, das Krankenhaus, die Praxis, die Krankenschwester
common_mistakes: Confusing Arzt (male) with Ärztin (female).

usage_notes: The feminine form of Arzt; used when the doctor is a woman.
common_collocations: zur Ärztin gehen, meine Ärztin
image_context: A female doctor in a white coat smiling at a patient
example_2: Die Ärztin untersucht den Patienten.
example_3: Ich habe einen Termin bei meiner Ärztin.
example_3_translation: Tenho uma consulta com minha médica.
pronunciation_tips: The umlaut 'Ä' is pronounced like the 'e' in 'bed'.
memory_hook: Like Arzt but with the feminine -in suffix, like 'doctorIN'.
related_words: der Arzt, die Praxis, das Krankenhaus, die Krankenschwester
common_mistakes: Using Arzt for a female doctor.

usage_notes: Compound of krank (sick) + Haus (house). Use 'ins Krankenhaus' (to the hospital).
common_collocations: ins Krankenhaus gehen, im Krankenhaus liegen, aus dem Krankenhaus kommen
image_context: A large hospital building with an emergency entrance
example_2: Das Krankenhaus ist in der Stadtmitte.
example_2_translation: O hospital fica no centro da cidade.
example_3: Sie liegt seit drei Tagen im Krankenhaus.
example_3_translation: Ela está internada no hospital há três dias.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: KRAN-ken-haus. The 'au' sounds like 'ow' in 'cow'.
memory_hook: A KRANKENHAUS is literally a 'sick house' — a house for sick people.
related_words: der Arzt, die Ärztin, die Notaufnahme, die Klinik
common_mistakes: Saying 'zum' instead of 'ins' (ins Krankenhaus, not zum Krankenhaus).

usage_notes: The everyday word for a medicine or drug prescribed by a doctor. Medizin is more general.
common_collocations: ein Medikament nehmen, Medikamente verschreiben, ein Medikament kaufen
image_context: Various prescription pills and a medicine bottle on a table
example_2: Ich nehme das Medikament dreimal am Tag.
example_2_translation: Tomo o medicamento três vezes ao dia.
example_3: Diese Medikamente sind sehr teuer.
example_3_translation: Esses medicamentos são muito caros.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on the last syllable: medi-ka-MENT.
memory_hook: MEDIKAMENT looks like 'medication' — same Latin root.
related_words: die Apotheke, das Rezept, die Tablette, die Medizin
common_mistakes: Using Medizin when referring to a specific pill or prescription.

secondary_meaning: medicine as a field of study
usage_notes: Can mean the field of medicine or colloquially refer to medicine/remedy.
common_collocations: Medizin studieren, traditionelle Medizin, Medizin nehmen
image_context: Medical books and a stethoscope representing the field of medicine
example_1: Er studiert Medizin an der Universität.
example_2: Nimm deine Medizin nach dem Essen.
example_2_translation: Tome seu remédio depois da refeição.
example_3: Die Medizin hilft gegen Kopfschmerzen.
example_3_translation: O remédio ajuda contra dor de cabeça.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on the final syllable: medi-TZIN. The 'z' is /t͡s/ not /z/.
memory_hook: MEDIZIN looks and sounds like 'medicine'.
related_words: das Medikament, der Arzt, die Apotheke, das Rezept
common_mistakes: Using Medizin for a specific prescribed medicine; prefer Medikament.

usage_notes: Almost always used in the plural in everyday speech: 'Ich habe Kopfschmerzen.'
common_collocations: Kopfschmerzen haben, Kopfschmerzen bekommen, starke Kopfschmerzen
image_context: A person clutching their head in pain due to a headache
example_1_translation: Tenho uma forte dor de cabeça.
example_2: Sie bekommt oft Kopfschmerzen bei der Arbeit.
example_2_translation: Ela frequentemente tem dor de cabeça no trabalho.
example_3: Das Medikament hilft gegen Kopfschmerzen.
example_3_translation: O remédio ajuda contra a dor de cabeça.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: KOPF (head) + SCHMERZEN (pains). Stress on 'Kopf'.
memory_hook: KOPF = head, SCHMERZEN = pains. Head-pains = headache.
related_words: der Kopf, der Schmerz, die Migräne, das Aspirin
common_mistakes: Using singular 'Kopfschmerz' in speech; plural is standard.

usage_notes: Often reflexive: 'sich fühlen' = to feel (a state). 'fühlen' alone = to physically touch/sense.
common_collocations: sich gut fühlen, sich schlecht fühlen, sich krank fühlen
image_context: A person touching their forehead to check for fever
example_1_translation: Como você está se sentindo hoje?
example_2: Ich fühle mich nicht gut.
example_3: Er fühlt sich nach der Medizin besser.
example_3_translation: Ele se sente melhor depois do remédio.
pronunciation_tips: The 'ü' is a rounded front vowel — round your lips as for 'oo' but say 'ee'.
memory_hook: FÜHLEN sounds like 'feeling' — to feel your emotions or physical state.
related_words: das Gefühl, empfinden, spüren, sich befinden
common_mistakes: Forgetting the reflexive pronoun: 'sich fühlen', not just 'fühlen'.

usage_notes: Usually uncountable. 'Fieber haben' = to have a fever.
common_collocations: Fieber haben, Fieber messen, hohes Fieber
image_context: A thermometer showing a high temperature next to a sick person
example_1_translation: A criança está com febre alta.
example_2: Hast du Fieber gemessen?
example_3: Der Arzt sagt, das Fieber ist nicht gefährlich.
example_3_translation: O médico diz que a febre não é perigosa.
pronunciation_tips: The final '-er' sounds like /ɐ/, a reduced schwa-like sound.
memory_hook: FIEBER sounds like 'fever' — they share the same Germanic root.
related_words: die Temperatur, das Thermometer, krank, der Schüttelfrost
common_mistakes: Using 'ein Fieber haben' with the indefinite article; standard is just 'Fieber haben'.

usage_notes: Plural 'Schmerzen' is used for ongoing or multiple pains. Often combined: Kopfschmerzen, Bauchschmerzen.
common_collocations: Schmerzen haben, starke Schmerzen, Schmerzen lindern
image_context: A person wincing and holding their side in pain
example_2: Der Schmerz ist sehr stark.
example_3: Das Medikament lindert die Schmerzen.
example_3_translation: O remédio alivia as dores.
pronunciation_tips: Initial 'Sch' sounds like English 'sh'; final 'z' is /t͡s/.
memory_hook: Think of someone saying 'SCHMERZ!' when they feel pain.
related_words: Kopfschmerzen, Bauchschmerzen, Rückenschmerzen, wehtun
common_mistakes: Confusing singular Schmerz (a single pain) with plural Schmerzen (pains, aches).

usage_notes: In Germany, pharmacies are strictly regulated and marked by a red 'A' sign.
common_collocations: in die Apotheke gehen, in der Apotheke kaufen
image_search_query: pharmacy drugstore red A sign Germany
image_context: A German pharmacy with the characteristic red 'A' sign
example_2: Diese Tabletten kaufe ich in der Apotheke.
example_3: Die Apotheke öffnet um acht Uhr.
example_3_translation: A farmácia abre às oito horas.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on the second syllable: a-PO-the-ke. Final 'e' is not silent.
memory_hook: APOTHEKE sounds like 'apothecary', the old word for pharmacist.
related_words: das Medikament, das Rezept, der Apotheker, die Tablette
common_mistakes: Confusing Apotheke (pharmacy) with Drogerie (drugstore selling non-prescription items).

usage_notes: Context determines meaning: medical context = prescription; cooking context = recipe.
common_collocations: ein Rezept bekommen, ein Rezept einlösen, auf Rezept
image_context: A doctor writing a medical prescription on paper
example_1: Der Arzt gibt mir ein Rezept.
example_1_translation: O médico me dá uma receita.
example_2: Ich hole das Rezept in der Apotheke ab.
example_3: Dieses Medikament bekommt man nur auf Rezept.
example_3_translation: Esse medicamento só se obtém com receita.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on the second syllable: re-ZEPT. The 'z' is /t͡s/.
memory_hook: REZEPT = prescription or recipe — a doctor's written instructions, like a recipe for getting better.
related_words: die Apotheke, das Medikament, verschreiben, der Arzt
common_mistakes: Using Rezept only for cooking — remember it means prescription in medical contexts.

usage_notes: A common cold; not to be confused with Grippe (flu), which is more severe.
common_collocations: eine Erkältung haben, eine Erkältung bekommen, an einer Erkältung leiden
image_context: A person blowing their nose with a tissue, looking cold and miserable
example_2: Im Winter bekommt man oft eine Erkältung.
example_2_translation: No inverno, as pessoas frequentemente ficam resfriadas.
example_3: Meine Erkältung dauert schon eine Woche.
example_3_translation: Meu resfriado já dura uma semana.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: er-KÄL-tung. The umlaut 'ä' sounds like 'e' in 'bed'.
memory_hook: ERKÄLTUNG comes from 'kalt' (cold) — you get an 'Erkältung' from the cold.
related_words: die Grippe, der Husten, der Schnupfen, krank, das Fieber
common_mistakes: Confusing Erkältung (cold) with Grippe (flu/influenza).

usage_notes: Noun (der Husten) and verb (husten) are both commonly used.
common_collocations: Husten haben, starker Husten, gegen Husten
image_context: A person covering their mouth while coughing
example_2: Der Husten kommt von der Erkältung.
example_3: Der Arzt gibt mir Tropfen gegen den Husten.
example_3_translation: O médico me dá gotas para a tosse.
pronunciation_tips: Initial 'H' is aspirated; 'u' is long /uː/; '-en' is a reduced /ən/.
memory_hook: HUSTEN sounds a bit like the action — a rough, forced breath.
related_words: die Erkältung, husten, der Hals, das Halsbonbon
common_mistakes: Using plural 'Husten' — it is almost always uncountable.

usage_notes: Opposite of krank. Used predicatively and attributively.
common_collocations: gesund sein, gesund werden, gesund bleiben, gesund essen
image_context: A smiling person exercising outdoors looking fit and healthy
example_2: Obst und Gemüse sind gesund.
example_3: Bleib gesund!
example_3_translation: Fique bem! / Cuide-se!
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: ge-SUND. Final 'd' is devoiced to /t/.
memory_hook: GESUND is the German word behind the toast 'Gesundheit!' when someone sneezes.
related_words: die Gesundheit, krank, fit, wohlauf, gesunden
common_mistakes: Forgetting adjective endings: ein gesunder Mann, eine gesunde Frau.

usage_notes: Always used in the plural in everyday speech, like Kopfschmerzen.
common_collocations: Bauchschmerzen haben, starke Bauchschmerzen, Bauchschmerzen bekommen
image_context: A person holding their abdomen in pain
example_2: Das Kind klagt über Bauchschmerzen.
example_2_translation: A criança está se queixando de dor de barriga.
example_3: Nach dem Essen hatte er starke Bauchschmerzen.
example_3_translation: Depois de comer, ele teve uma forte dor de barriga.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: BAUCH (belly/stomach) + SCHMERZEN (pains). Stress on 'Bauch'.
memory_hook: BAUCH = belly, SCHMERZEN = pains. Belly-pains = stomachache.
related_words: der Bauch, der Schmerz, Kopfschmerzen, Rückenschmerzen
common_mistakes: Using the singular 'Bauchschmerz' — the plural is standard in everyday speech.

usage_notes: In a medical context, einen Termin machen/haben = to make/have an appointment.
common_collocations: einen Termin machen, einen Termin haben, einen Termin absagen
image_context: A calendar with a doctor appointment marked
example_1: Ich mache einen Termin beim Arzt.
example_1_translation: Estou marcando uma consulta com o médico.
example_2: Mein Termin ist um zehn Uhr.
example_2_translation: Minha consulta é às dez horas.
example_3: Ich muss den Termin leider absagen.
example_3_translation: Infelizmente preciso cancelar a consulta.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on the second syllable: ter-MIN. The 'r' is uvular /ʁ/.
memory_hook: A TERMIN is like a 'terminal point' — the fixed time you must arrive.
related_words: der Arzt, die Ärztin, die Praxis, vereinbaren
common_mistakes: Translating directly from English 'appointment' — Termin is the correct German word.

usage_notes: Common in medical discussions. 'eine Allergie gegen etwas haben' = to be allergic to something.
common_collocations: eine Allergie haben, gegen etwas allergisch sein, die Pollenallergie
image_context: A person sneezing outside during spring due to pollen allergies
example_1: Ich habe eine Allergie gegen Katzen.
example_2: Sie hat eine Pollenallergie.
example_3: Der Arzt hat meine Allergie getestet.
example_3_translation: O médico testou minha alergia.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable: al-ler-GIE. The long /iː/ ending is like English 'ee'.
memory_hook: ALLERGIE is nearly identical to English 'allergy' — same spelling, same meaning.
related_words: allergisch, die Pollenallergie, das Antihistaminikum, niesen
common_mistakes: Using 'allergisch gegen' instead of the more natural 'eine Allergie gegen haben'.

usage_notes: Refers specifically to a tablet or pill form of medication.
common_collocations: eine Tablette nehmen, Tabletten schlucken, Tabletten verschreiben
image_context: A hand holding white tablets or pills
example_1: Ich nehme zwei Tabletten am Tag.
example_2: Diese Tabletten helfen gegen Schmerzen.
example_2_translation: Esses comprimidos ajudam contra a dor.
example_3: Die Ärztin verschreibt mir Tabletten.
example_3_translation: A médica me prescreve comprimidos.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: ta-BLET-te. Final 'e' is pronounced /ə/.
memory_hook: TABLETTE looks like 'tablet' — a tablet-shaped pill.
related_words: das Medikament, die Kapsel, die Pille, das Rezept
common_mistakes: Forgetting the feminine article: die Tablette.

usage_notes: Mandatory in Germany. GKV (public) and PKV (private) are the two types.
common_collocations: eine Krankenversicherung haben, gesetzliche Krankenversicherung
image_context: A German health insurance card (Krankenversicherungskarte)
example_2: Die Krankenversicherung bezahlt das Medikament.
example_2_translation: O plano de saúde paga o medicamento.
example_3: In Deutschland hat jeder eine Krankenversicherung.
example_3_translation: Na Alemanha, todo mundo tem plano de saúde.
pronunciation_tips: A long compound word: KRANKEN-VER-SICHE-RUNG. Stress on first syllable.
memory_hook: KRANKEN (sick people) + VERSICHERUNG (insurance) = insurance for sick people.
related_words: das Krankenhaus, der Arzt, die Versicherung, die Krankenkasse
common_mistakes: Mispronouncing — take it one component at a time: Kranken-versicherung.

usage_notes: Hoher Blutdruck = high blood pressure (hypertension); niedriger Blutdruck = low blood pressure.
common_collocations: den Blutdruck messen, hoher Blutdruck, niedriger Blutdruck
image_context: A blood pressure cuff being used to measure blood pressure
example_1_translation: O médico mede minha pressão arterial.
example_2: Ich habe hohen Blutdruck.
example_3: Stress kann den Blutdruck erhöhen.
example_3_translation: O estresse pode aumentar a pressão arterial.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: BLUT (blood) + DRUCK (pressure). Stress on first syllable.
memory_hook: BLUT = blood, DRUCK = pressure/print. Blood-pressure.
related_words: das Blut, der Druck, messen, das Herz
common_mistakes: Using 'der Blutdruck' inconsistently — it is always masculine.

usage_notes: In a medical context, refers to a physical exam or diagnostic procedure.
common_collocations: eine Untersuchung machen, zur Untersuchung gehen, ärztliche Untersuchung
image_context: A doctor examining a patient in a clinical setting
example_2: Ich habe eine ärztliche Untersuchung morgen.
example_3: Die Untersuchung dauert etwa eine Stunde.
example_3_translation: O exame dura cerca de uma hora.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: un-ter-ZU-chung. The 'ch' is /x/, not /k/ or /tʃ/.
memory_hook: Think of 'under-searching' — a doctor thoroughly searching under the surface.
related_words: untersuchen, der Arzt, die Diagnose, der Befund
common_mistakes: Mispronouncing 'ch' as /k/ — it is /x/ (like Scottish 'loch').

usage_notes: Colloquially shortened to 'OP' in German medical contexts.
common_collocations: eine Operation machen, operiert werden, vor der Operation
image_context: Surgeons performing an operation in an operating room
example_1: Er braucht eine Operation am Herz.
example_1_translation: Ele precisa de uma cirurgia no coração.
example_2: Die Operation war erfolgreich.
example_3: Nach der Operation muss sie sich erholen.
example_3_translation: Depois da operação, ela precisa se recuperar.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on the last syllable: ope-ra-TSION. The 'ti' is /t͡si̯/.
memory_hook: OPERATION is the same word in English and German — just pronounced differently.
related_words: operieren, der Chirurg, das Krankenhaus, die Narkose
common_mistakes: Stress placement — in German the stress falls on the final syllable, unlike English.

usage_notes: Literally 'sick-sister'. The modern gender-neutral term is Pflegefachkraft. Male nurse = Krankenpfleger.
common_collocations: die Krankenschwester rufen, mit der Krankenschwester sprechen
image_context: A female nurse in a hospital uniform assisting a patient
example_2: Ich möchte Krankenschwester werden.
example_3: Die Krankenschwester misst mein Fieber.
example_3_translation: A enfermeira mede minha febre.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: KRANKEN (sick) + SCHWESTER (sister). Stress on first component.
memory_hook: A KRANKENSCHWESTER is literally a 'sister to the sick' — a caring nurse.
related_words: der Krankenpfleger, der Arzt, das Krankenhaus, die Pflege
common_mistakes: Using Krankenschwester for a male nurse — use Krankenpfleger instead.

usage_notes: From impfen (to vaccinate). Impfpass = vaccination record book.
common_collocations: eine Impfung bekommen, zur Impfung gehen, die Grippeimpfung
image_context: A syringe giving a vaccination injection into an arm
example_1: Ich brauche eine Impfung gegen Grippe.
example_1_translation: Preciso de uma vacina contra gripe.
example_2: Die Impfung schützt vor der Krankheit.
example_2_translation: A vacina protege contra a doença.
example_3: Haben Sie Ihren Impfpass dabei?
example_3_translation: Você trouxe sua carteira de vacinação?
pronunciation_tips: The 'pf' cluster is a single affricate /pf/ — say 'p' and 'f' together.
memory_hook: IMPFUNG = vaccination — the 'pf' sound is distinctly German.
related_words: impfen, der Impfpass, der Impfstoff, die Grippe
common_mistakes: Pronouncing 'pf' as just 'f' — both sounds must be articulated.
